title: Fractions as division
description: Practice understanding that the fraction bar really means division.
topic_path: Get ready for negative number operations

assessment_items.question_content:
**Drag the cards below to create an expression that is equal to $\dfrac65$.**  


[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: A fraction can be read as $\goldE{\text{numerator}} \div \blueE{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\goldE6}{\blueE5}=\goldE6\div\blueE5$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Drag the cards below to create an expression that is equal to $\dfrac72$.**  

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\greenD7}{\blueD2}=\greenD7\div\blueD2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Drag the cards below to create an expression that is equal to $\dfrac16$.**  

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\greenD1}{\blueD6}=\greenD1\div\blueD6$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Drag the cards below to create an expression that is equal to $\dfrac4{12}$.**  


[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\greenD4}{\blueD{12}}=\greenD4\div\blueD{12}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**The fraction $\dfrac{x}{y}$ must be equal to which expression?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac\greenD{x}\blueD{y}=\greenD{x}\div\blueD{y}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**The fraction $\dfrac{a}{b}$ must be equal to which expression?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac\greenD{a}\blueD{b}=\greenD{a}\div\blueD{b}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**The fraction $\dfrac{r}{s}$ must be equal to which expression?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac\greenD{r}\blueD{s}=\greenD{r}\div\blueD{s}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**The fraction $\dfrac{\ell}{m}$ must be equal to which expression?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac\greenD{\ell}\blueD{m}=\greenD{\ell}\div\blueD{m}$
A division problem can be rewritten as a fraction:  $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\greenD2\div\blueD7=\dfrac{\greenD2}{\blueD7}$
A division problem can be rewritten as a fraction:  $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\greenD3\div\blueD{11}=\dfrac{\greenD3}{\blueD{11}}$
A division problem can be rewritten as a fraction:  $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\greenD{10}\div\blueD{4}=\dfrac{\greenD{10}}{\blueD{4}}$
A division problem can be rewritten as a fraction:  $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\greenD{8}\div\blueD{5}=\dfrac{\greenD{8}}{\blueD{5}}$
A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\greenD9}{\blueD4}=\greenD9\div\blueD4$
A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\greenD5}{\blueD9}=\greenD5\div\blueD9$
A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\greenD3}{\blueD{10}}=\greenD3\div\blueD{10}$
A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\greenD7}{\blueD3}=\greenD7\div\blueD3$
A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\greenD9}{\blueD8}={\greenD9}\div{\blueD8}$
A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\greenD5}{\blueD2}={\greenD5}\div{\blueD2}$
A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\greenD4}{\blueD6}={\greenD4}\div{\blueD6}$
A fraction can be read as $\greenD{\text{numerator}} \div \blueD{\text{denominator}}$.
$\dfrac{\greenD6}{\blueD7}={\greenD6}\div{\blueD7}$
